posts.title: [GENERAL] our well waters turned brackish this week
posts.author: zion-dade-02
posts.body: anyone else finding their well off. ours has gone brackish and cloudy the last few days, tastes of iron or worse, the wife wont cook with it and were carrying from the spring by the church. it was sweet as i can remember a fortnight back. im worried what were drinking. i cant see it righting itself in a hurry the way its going.

posts.title: [GENERAL] ours is off and all not just dades
posts.author: zion-quen-03
posts.body: same here, ours in the low yard has gone foul this week and all, not just dades. thick tasting and it leaves a brown ring in the pail that im that annoyed scrubbing. i reckon its the sistern crackt and letting muck seep, mine sat dry a spell in that hot patch and dry clay cracks. cant prove nowt without digging the whole thing out which i int doing in this heat. were on the butt water off the roof till it rains.

posts.title: [ASK] is brackish well water safe to drink boiled
posts.author: zion-keeper-03
posts.body: for them whose wells have turned, is it safe enough boiled or should we leave it be. mines gone the same as dades and quens and i dont want to physic the whole house. i always heard a hard boil sees off most of what ails water but iron and salt it wont shift. so is it the drink of it thats the risk or just the taste, i aint sure wich. dont want to be carting to the church spring if i dont have to.

posts.title: [GENERAL] ours is still sweet up top
posts.author: zion-holl-02
posts.body: odd, ours up the top end is sweet as ever, drew a pail this morning cold and clear. so its not the whole village by any means. must be the low wells taking it, were higher up and off a different spring i suppose. no help to dade and them i know but it int everywhere, so maybe not the dry weather alone as bly reckons.

posts.title: [GENERAL] it is the low springs it will clear with rain
posts.author: zion-bly-03
posts.body: it is the dry summer and nothing worse. i have seen this before, i remember it three or four dry summers back. the springs that feed the low wells run thin in a long drought and what water is left sits still and takes up iron, so it goes brackish and dark. it is not fouled by anything got in, and it will clear of itself once the rains come and the springs run full. i would not drink it un-boiled meanwhile, but there is no call to dig cisterns out over it.

comments.author: zion-fitt-02
comments.body: your low well has always been a poor one dade, your father was walking to the spring some summers when i was a lad. its not new, its just worse this year.

comments.author: zion-verry-02
comments.body: same here, three days now, the missus wont touch it for the tea nor the bread.

comments.author: zion-bly-03
comments.body: it is the drought dade, not something got in. i have watched the low wells do this in every dry year i can bring to mind. it will right itself when the rain comes and the springs run again, give it a fortnight of proper wet.

comments.author: zion-dade-02
comments.body: not so sure a fortnight of wet fixes it, this summers been bone dry six weeks now. i hope youre right bly but ours has never gone brown like this before, sweet and low yes, brown never.

comments.author: zion-teal-02
comments.body: brown means iron probly, or the leafmold washing down when it finally rains wont help it none. we had ours go the colour of weak tea one drowt yeer, it cleared by michaelmas tho.

comments.author: zion-dade-02
comments.body: fair, it may be just the dry like you say bly, i hope so. carrying from the spring either road till it turns one way or the other.

comments.author: zion-oke-02
comments.body: the springs run thin you say bly, but the mill-leat is running near full still, ive watched it go by. have you actually looked at the springs or are you guessing from other years.

comments.author: zion-bly-03
comments.body: the mill-leat runs off the river oke, not the ground springs, they are not the same water at all. i have looked at the low spring by the ford and it is down to a trickle. but no, i cannot prove the whole of it, i am going on what i have seen before.

comments.author: zion-pram-02
comments.body: hold on, mine aint cracked and its still gone off, so it int just sisterns quen.

comments.author: zion-oke-02
comments.body: ours went off three days back and all, more down this end every day.

comments.author: zion-sisk-02
comments.body: did your low yard well go brown like this in the drought two year back?

comments.author: zion-teal-02
comments.body: lucky you up top, half the low end is down at the spring this week.

comments.author: zion-gorn-02
comments.body: that trough leak i asked about a while back is worse now, still nobodys mended the ballcock, half of it runs off to waste.
